sentences.sentence: Up to the middle of the 18th century, the navigators were still unable to exactly identify the position at sea, so they might face a great number of risks such as the shipwreck or running out of supplies before arriving at the destination.
sentences.translation: 直到 18 世纪中叶，航海者仍无法准确确定海上位置，因此可能面临船只失事、抵达目的地前耗尽补给等诸多风险。

structure.text: Up to the middle of the 18th century
the navigators were still unable to exactly identify the position at sea
so they might face a great number of risks such as the shipwreck or running out of supplies before arriving at the destination

difficult_points: so 连接无法定位这一原因与航海风险这一结果。
running out 与 the shipwreck 并列作 such as 后的例子；before arriving 的逻辑主语是 navigators。

sentences.sentence: Knowing one's position on the earth requires two simple but essential coordinates, one of which is the longitude.
sentences.translation: 要知道自己在地球上的位置，需要两个简单却不可或缺的坐标，其中一个就是经度。

structure.text: Knowing one's position on the earth
requires two simple but essential coordinates
one of which is the longitude

difficult_points: Knowing ... 是动名词短语作主语。
one of which 中 which 指 two ... coordinates。

sentences.sentence: The longitude is a term that can be used to measure the distance that one has covered from one's home to another place around the world without the limitations of naturally occurring baseline like the equator.
sentences.translation: 经度这一概念可用于衡量一个人从原地到世界另一地点所跨越的距离，而不受赤道这类天然基线的限制。

structure.text: The longitude is a term
that can be used to measure the distance
that one has covered from one's home to another place around the world without the limitations of naturally occurring baseline like the equator

difficult_points: 两个 that 从句分别修饰 term 和 distance，层级不同。
without ... 表示这种衡量不依赖赤道之类的天然基线；baseline 前原文缺冠词或复数标记。

sentences.sentence: To determine longitude, navigators had no choice but to measure the angle with the naval sextant between Moon centre and a specific star— lunar distance— along with the height of both heavenly bodies.
sentences.translation: 为了确定经度，航海者只能用航海六分仪测量月球中心与某颗特定恒星之间的夹角——即月距——并测量两个天体的高度。

structure.text: To determine longitude
navigators had no choice but to measure the angle with the naval sextant
between Moon centre and a specific star— lunar distance— along with the height of both heavenly bodies

difficult_points: had no choice but to 表示“别无选择，只能”。
lunar distance 是对前述夹角的破折号同位解释；along with 又加入两个天体的高度。

sentences.sentence: Together with the nautical almanac, Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) was determined, which could be adopted to calculate longitude because one hour in GMT means 15-degree longitude.
sentences.translation: 借助航海历可以确定格林尼治标准时间（GMT），再用它计算经度，因为 GMT 相差一小时就对应 15 度经度。

structure.text: Together with the nautical almanac
Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) was determined
which could be adopted to calculate longitude because one hour in GMT means 15-degree longitude

difficult_points: which 指 Greenwich Mean Time，说明它可用于计算经度。
because 从句解释时间差能够换算经度的原因。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, this approach laid great reliance on the weather conditions, which brought great inconvenience to the crew members.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，这种方法高度依赖天气条件，给船员带来了很大不便。

structure.text: Unfortunately
this approach laid great reliance on the weather conditions
which brought great inconvenience to the crew members

difficult_points: which 可理解为指天气依赖这一整件事。
laid great reliance on 搭配生硬，通常说 placed great reliance on 或 relied heavily on。

sentences.sentence: Therefore, another method was proposed, that is, the time difference between the home time and the local time served for the measurement.
sentences.translation: 因此，人们提出了另一种方法，即利用出发地时间与当地时间之差进行测量。

structure.text: Therefore, another method was proposed
that is
the time difference between the home time and the local time served for the measurement

difficult_points: that is 引出对 another method 的具体解释。
served for the measurement 表示时间差用于测量，但表达不如 served as the basis for measurement 自然。

sentences.sentence: Theoretically, knowing the longitude position was quite simple, even for the people in the middle of the sea with no land in sight.
sentences.translation: 理论上，确定经度位置相当简单，即使人在茫茫大海中、视野内完全看不到陆地也是如此。

structure.text: Theoretically
knowing the longitude position
was quite simple, even for the people in the middle of the sea with no land in sight

difficult_points: knowing ... 是动名词短语作主语。
with no land in sight 是 with 复合结构，描述看不到陆地的处境。

expressions.text: in the middle of the sea

sentences.sentence: The key element for calculating the distance travelled was to know, at the very moment, the accurate home time.
sentences.translation: 计算航行距离的关键，是在当下准确知道出发地时间。

structure.text: The key element for calculating the distance travelled
was to know, at the very moment, the accurate home time

difficult_points: travelled 是过去分词，后置修饰 distance。
to know ... 是不定式作表语；at the very moment 是插入的时间状语。

sentences.sentence: But the greatest problem is: how can a sailor know the home time at sea?
sentences.translation: 但最大的问题是：水手在海上怎样才能知道出发地的时间？

structure.text: But the greatest problem is
how can a sailor know the home time at sea

difficult_points: 冒号后的直接疑问句说明 greatest problem 的内容。
home time 指船只出发地或基准地点的时间。

sentences.sentence: The simple and again obvious answer is that one takes an accurate clock with him, which he sets to the home time before leaving.
sentences.translation: 简单而又显而易见的答案是：带上一只准确的钟，并在出发前把它调到出发地时间。

structure.text: The simple and again obvious answer
is that one takes an accurate clock with him
which he sets to the home time before leaving

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，说明答案内容。
which 指 an accurate clock；before leaving 省略主语 one。

sentences.sentence: A comparison with the local time (easily identified by checking the position of the Sun) would indicate the time difference between the home time and the local time, and thus the distance from home was obtained.
sentences.translation: 将其与当地时间比较——当地时间很容易通过观察太阳位置确定——就能得出出发地与当地的时差，从而算出离出发地的距离。

structure.text: A comparison with the local time (easily identified by checking the position of the Sun)
would indicate the time difference between the home time and the local time
and thus the distance from home was obtained

difficult_points: easily identified ... 是过去分词短语，补充说明 local time。
前半使用 would indicate，后半突然改用 was obtained，时态语气不完全一致但原文保留。

sentences.sentence: The truth was that nobody in the 18th century had ever managed to create a clock that could endure the violent shaking of a ship and the fluctuating temperature while still maintaining the accuracy of time for navigation.
sentences.translation: 事实是，18 世纪还没有人成功制造出一种钟，既能承受船只的剧烈摇晃和温度波动，又能保持航海计时所需的精度。

structure.text: The truth was
that nobody in the 18th century had ever managed to create a clock
that could endure the violent shaking of a ship and the fluctuating temperature while still maintaining the accuracy of time for navigation

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导表语从句，第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 clock。
while still maintaining 表示承受外界影响的同时仍保持精度，逻辑主语是 clock。

sentences.sentence: After 1714, as an attempt to find a solution to the problem, the British government offered a tremendous amount of £20,000, which were to be managed by the magnificently named'Board of Longitude'.
sentences.translation: 1714 年后，为了尝试解决这一问题，英国政府拿出高达 2 万英镑的奖金，由名字颇为气派的“经度委员会”管理。

structure.text: After 1714, as an attempt to find a solution to the problem
the British government offered a tremendous amount of £20,000
which were to be managed by the magnificently named'Board of Longitude'

difficult_points: which 指 £20,000 这笔款项；were 与 amount 的单数形式存在主谓一致问题。
named 与开引号之间缺少空格，属于排版异常。

sentences.sentence: If timekeeper was the answer (and there could be other proposed solutions, since the money wasn't only offered for timekeeper), then the error of the required timekeeping for achieving this goal needed to be within 2.8 seconds a day, which was considered impossible for any clock or watch at sea, even when they were in their finest conditions.
sentences.translation: 如果计时器就是答案——当然也可能有其他方案，因为奖金并非只为计时器而设——那么要实现目标，计时误差必须控制在每天 2.8 秒以内；这在当时被认为是任何海上钟表都不可能达到的，即使设备处于最佳状态。

structure.text: If timekeeper was the answer (and there could be other proposed solutions, since the money wasn't only offered for timekeeper)
then the error of the required timekeeping for achieving this goal needed to be within 2.8 seconds a day
which was considered impossible for any clock or watch at sea, even when they were in their finest conditions

difficult_points: 括号内 and 分句补充说明奖金也接受其他解决方案，since 再解释原因。
which 指每天误差不超过 2.8 秒这一要求；they 指 clocks or watches。

expressions.meaning_zh: 每天误差在 2.8 秒以内

sentences.sentence: This award, worth about £2 million today, inspired the self-taught Yorkshire carpenter John Harrison to attempt a design for a practical marine clock.
sentences.translation: 这笔奖金按今天价值约合 200 万英镑，它激励了约克郡自学成才的木匠约翰·哈里森尝试设计一款实用航海钟。

structure.text: This award, worth about £2 million today
inspired the self-taught Yorkshire carpenter John Harrison
to attempt a design for a practical marine clock

difficult_points: worth ... 是形容词短语，补充说明 award 的现今价值。
inspire + 人 + to do 表示激励某人做某事。

sentences.sentence: In the later stage of his early career, he worked alongside his younger brother James.
sentences.translation: 在早期事业的后半阶段，他与弟弟詹姆斯并肩工作。

structure.text: In the later stage of his early career
he
worked alongside his younger brother James

difficult_points: he 指 John Harrison。
James 是 his younger brother 的同位说明。

sentences.sentence: The first big project of theirs was to build a turret clock for the stables at Brockelsby Park, which was revolutionary because it required no lubrication.
sentences.translation: 兄弟俩的第一个大型项目，是为布罗克尔斯比公园的马厩建造一座塔钟；它无需润滑，因此具有革命性。

structure.text: The first big project of theirs
was to build a turret clock for the stables at Brockelsby Park
which was revolutionary because it required no lubrication

difficult_points: to build ... 是不定式作表语，说明 project 内容。
which 指 turret clock；because 从句说明其革命性的原因。

sentences.sentence: Harrison designed a marine clock in 1730, and he travelled to London in seek of financial aid.
sentences.translation: 1730 年，哈里森设计了一款航海钟，并前往伦敦寻求资金援助。

structure.text: Harrison designed a marine clock in 1730
and he travelled to London in seek of financial aid

difficult_points: and 连接设计航海钟与赴伦敦求助两个动作。
in seek of 搭配错误，通常应为 in search of 或 seeking；原文保留。

sentences.sentence: He explained his ideas to Edmond Halley, the Astronomer Royal, who then introduced him to George Graham, Britain's first-class clockmaker.
sentences.translation: 他向皇家天文学家埃德蒙·哈雷说明自己的构想；哈雷随后把他介绍给英国一流钟表匠乔治·格雷厄姆。

structure.text: He explained his ideas to Edmond Halley, the Astronomer Royal
who then introduced him to George Graham, Britain's first-class clockmaker

difficult_points: the Astronomer Royal 是 Edmond Halley 的同位语。
who 指 Halley；him 指 Harrison；Britain's first-class clockmaker 是 George Graham 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: Graham provided him with financial aid for his early-stage work on sea clocks.
sentences.translation: 格雷厄姆为他早期的航海钟研究提供了资金支持。

structure.text: Graham
provided him with financial aid
for his early-stage work on sea clocks

difficult_points: him 和 his 均指 Harrison。
provide someone with something 是双宾语意义结构。

sentences.sentence: It took Harrison five years to build Harrison Number One or HI.
sentences.translation: 哈里森花了五年时间制造“哈里森一号”，即 H1。

structure.text: It
took Harrison five years
to build Harrison Number One or HI

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，to build ... 是实际内容。
HI 很可能是 H1 的 OCR 误识别；原文按 HI 保留。

sentences.sentence: Later, he sought the improvement from alternate design and produced H4 with the giant clock appearance.
sentences.translation: 后来，他通过替代设计寻求改进，并制造出外观如大型钟表的 H4。

structure.text: Later
he sought the improvement from alternate design
and produced H4 with the giant clock appearance

difficult_points: he 指 Harrison；sought 与 produced 是并列谓语。
sought the improvement from alternate design 和 giant clock appearance 表达生硬，翻译按可辨语义处理。

sentences.sentence: Remarkable as it was, the Board of Longitude wouldn't grant him the prize for some time until it was adequately satisfied.
sentences.translation: 尽管成果非凡，经度委员会仍有一段时间不肯把奖金授予他，直到委员会充分满意为止。

structure.text: Remarkable as it was
the Board of Longitude wouldn't grant him the prize for some time
until it was adequately satisfied

difficult_points: 形容词 + as + 主语 + 系动词是让步倒装，意为“尽管它很出色”。
until 从句中的 it 在语义上指 Board of Longitude，而非作品。

sentences.sentence: Harrison had a principal contestant for the tempting prize at that time, an English mathematician called John Hadley, who developed the sextant.
sentences.translation: 当时，哈里森争夺这笔诱人奖金的主要竞争者是英国数学家约翰·哈德利；他发明了六分仪。

structure.text: Harrison had a principal contestant for the tempting prize at that time
an English mathematician called John Hadley
who developed the sextant

difficult_points: an English mathematician ... 是 contestant 的同位语。
who 指 John Hadley；contestant 在语境中实际表示 competitor。

sentences.sentence: The sextant is the tool that people adopt to measure angles, such as the one between the Sun and the horizon, for a calculation of the location of ships or planes.
sentences.translation: 六分仪是一种测量角度的工具，例如测量太阳与地平线之间的夹角，以计算船只或飞机的位置。

structure.text: The sextant is the tool
that people adopt to measure angles, such as the one between the Sun and the horizon
for a calculation of the location of ships or planes

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 tool；to measure 表示采用该工具的目的。
the one 指 angle，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: In addition, his invention is significant since it can help determine longitude.
sentences.translation: 此外，他的发明意义重大，因为它能帮助确定经度。

structure.text: In addition
his invention is significant
since it can help determine longitude

difficult_points: his invention 和 it 都指 Hadley 的六分仪。
help determine 采用 help + 动词原形结构。

sentences.sentence: Most chronometer forerunners of that particular generation were English, but that doesn't mean every achievement was made by them.
sentences.translation: 那个特定时代的大多数精密计时器先驱都是英国人，但这并不意味着所有成就都由英国人取得。

structure.text: Most chronometer forerunners of that particular generation were English
but that doesn't mean every achievement was made by them

difficult_points: that 指前半句“大多数先驱是英国人”这一事实。
every achievement was made by them 是 mean 后省略 that 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: One wonderful figure in the history is the Lancastrian Thomas Earnshaw, who created the ultimate form of chronometer escapement—the spring detent escapement— and made the final decision on format and productions system for the marine chronometer, which turns it into a genuine modem commercial product, as well as a safe and pragmatic way of navigation at sea over the next century and half.
sentences.translation: 这段历史中的杰出人物之一，是兰开夏人托马斯·恩肖。他创造了精密计时器擒纵机构的最终形式——弹簧止动擒纵机构——并最终确定了航海天文钟的样式与生产体系，使其成为真正的现代商业产品，也成为此后一个半世纪安全而实用的海上导航手段。

structure.text: One wonderful figure in the history is the Lancastrian Thomas Earnshaw
who created the ultimate form of chronometer escapement—the spring detent escapement— and made the final decision on format and productions system for the marine chronometer
which turns it into a genuine modem commercial product, as well as a safe and pragmatic way of navigation at sea over the next century and half

difficult_points: who 指 Thomas Earnshaw，created 与 made 是并列谓语；破折号中内容解释 escapement 的具体形式。
which 指前述设计与生产体系的确立；modem 很可能是 modern 的 OCR 错误，productions system 和 century and half 也有语法异常。

expressions.text: a safe and pragmatic way of
